reasoning:
Zohran Mamdani is the Democratic nominee in the November 2025 NYC general mayoral election. In a heavily Democratic city, the Democratic nominee is practically guaranteed to win first place, unless there are unique confounding factors. 

In this case, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa are also in the race. Recent independent polling (October 17-20, 2025) shows Mamdani holding a massive double-digit lead over Cuomo. AARP/Gotham and Fox News polling put Mamdani consistently around 43-52%, while Cuomo is in the high 20s and Sliwa is around 14-20%. The only plausible way Mamdani loses (and gets second place) is if Sliwa drops out and endorses Cuomo, consolidating the anti-Mamdani vote into a single opponent. However, multiple sources from late October (Oct 21-23) confirm that Sliwa "under no circumstance" intends to drop out and has repeatedly rebuked pressure to do so.

Even if Sliwa were to drop out at the final moment, head-to-head polls indicate Mamdani would still be slightly favored or roughly tied against Cuomo (44.6% to 40.7% in AARP). Since Sliwa remains firmly in the race and early voting begins soon, Mamdani's path to victory is highly secure. Historically, a candidate leading by 20 points in late October has an extremely low probability of losing. Since scoring second place requires Mamdani to *lose* the mayoral race, the probability is approximately equal to his chances of being upset, which is conservatively around 3-5%.
